item.title: Visiting Italy: Cinque terre
item.description: The Cinque Terre are five coastal villages in the province of La Spezia. “Discovered” by foreigners in the late 20th century they have become among the most touristed areas of Italy; Monterosso al Mare Vernazza Corniglia Manarola Riomaggiore View Slideshow 1 2 ► [Parco Nazionale delle Cinque Terre][1] Riomaggiore is situated in a small valley just along the coast. It is the first Village of Cinque Terre when you come from La Spezia.

item.title: Visiting Italy: portovenere
item.description: The Ligurian coast between Cinque Terre and Portovenere is a cultural landscape of great scenic and cultural value. The layout and disposition of the small towns and the shaping of the surrounding landscape, overcoming the disadvantages of a steep, uneven terrain, encapsulate the continuous history of human settlement in this region over the past millennium. tower-houses The Castle, S. Lawrence (16th century) **Catholic church in Portovenere** Used to be a temple to Venus Erycina (From which the name Portus Veneris derives).

item.title: Visiting Italy: Milano
item.description: Arch of peace **Castello sforzesco** Arriving from the duomo di Milano, The arche of peace, in the parc behind the castle Basilica san-lorenzo maggiore Statue of Constantine, located in the plaza in front of the basilica Church Santa Maria Delle Grazie La Scala de Milan Planetarium The planetarium, which is one of the most-renowned in Europe ## A church

item.title: Visiting Italy: Genova
item.description: Genova city, San Lorenzo church and the seaport… The port of Genoa is the first in Italy. It ranks second in the Mediterranean after Marseille, France. The Aquarium of Genoa is the largest in Europe. many companies have their boat here Famous boat from the movie “Hook” Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova Italie Genova

item.title: Visiting Italy: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II
item.description: The Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II is a covered arcade situated on the northern side of the Piazza del Duomo in Milan, connecting to the Piazza della Scala. Named after King Victor Emmanuel II (It. Vittorio Emanuele II), the first king of united Italy, it was built by G. Mengoni between 1865 and 1878. The street is covered over by an arching glass and steel roof, thus giving it a claim to be the world ’s first

item.title: Visiting Italy: Duomo di Milano
item.description: The Duomo di Milano is one of the most famous buildings in Europe. It is the second largest Roman Catholic cathedral: only the cathedral of Seville is larger (as is Saint Peter ’s Basilica in Rome, which is not a cathedral). It is 157 meters long 40,000 people can fit comfortably within. The main spire is 109 meters high. The great windows of the choir were reputed to be the largest in the world.

item.title: Visiting Italy: Portofino
item.description: Portofino has a Mediterranean climate with hot and dry summers. Even in Winter the weather is never cold. “A little village stretching like an arch of the moon around a quiet basin. Never have I felt the way I did when I walked into that green indefiniteness, with such a sense of peace and fulfilment”. From Vie Errante by Guy de Maupassant _Portofino Population: 35,254 Italy Population: 57,194,000 Currency: Euro Time Zone: GMT+1 Language: Italian_

item.title: Visiting Italy: Pisa
item.description: Pisa is a quite small city, if you go there using the train, and want to see the leaning tower, you ’ll have to walk straight away during 20 minutes to reach: The Square of miracle (The Campo dei Miracoli ) The Square of miracle is made of four buildings: The Leaning tower, The Cathedral, The Baptistry, The Camposanto. ## La Torre di Pisa italie italie italie italie italie Characteristics of the leaning Tower:
